title: Default Field Initialization in Java Classes

source.submodule: Classes, Objects and Methods in Java

topicTags: Classes, Objects and Methods in Java

content.problemStatement:
In Java, **instance variables** (fields) are automatically initialized with default values if they are not explicitly assigned a value. The default values depend on the data type of the field.

**Syntax**
```java
int number;      // Default value is 0
boolean isActive;  // Default value is false
String name;     // Default value is null
```
---

**Example:**
```java
class Seedit {
    // Instance variables (fields)
    int number;      // Default value is 0
    boolean isActive;  // Default value is false
    String name;     // Default value is null

    public void displayValues() {
        System.out.println("Number: " + number);      // Prints: 0
        System.out.println("Is Active: " + isActive);  // Prints: false
        System.out.println("Name: " + name);          // Prints: null
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SEED-IT obj = new SEED-IT();

        // Call method to display default values
        obj.displayValues();
    }
}
```
---

**Expected Output:**
```default
Number: 0
Is Active: false
Name: null
```
